title: Conquering Old Age and Death | BG 7.27 - 7.30 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Okay, conquering old age and death.
So it's based on the teachings of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada.
We'll start with the 27th verse and we'll chant this verse together.
O Saiyan of Bharata, Arjuna, O conqueror of the four,
all living entities are born into delusion,
overcome by dualities of desire and hate.
We have covered this earlier also.

This doubt comes in their mind.

Why? Why has God created this world?
A very fundamental, we are discussing this philosophy, Gita, we are talking about God.
A very fundamental, we are living in this world, this world intrinsically has lot of pain and suffering.
Although we may be optimist, we can say that no, we have to look at the brighter side of things.
We should be positive, positive thinking, all this is great, all this is fine.
Intrinsically speaking, why there is suffering in this world?
If you have good karma, will we not die?
So, I personally feel that when you are in pain, you are closest to God.
Okay, let me ask you another very fundamental question before we discuss.
How many of us feel, how many of us feel that this world is a good place, a wonderful place, nice place?

And opinions are based on our experiences.

Or we haven't encountered some very, you know, painful period of time in our lives.
Then we might opine that this world is a wonderful place.

No proper place to stay, no proper food to eat and, you know, somehow grew up.

And in the Gita, Krishna talks about that this world is
Dukkhalayam means a place of misery.

Which treatment can he not afford?

we are governed by the three modes of material nature.

So there was a king who was living in a palace

very close to his palace there was a hut.

He would be sleeping on the floor.
When it rains the whole roof will be dripping.

there was a big smile on his face.

Persons who have acted piously in previous life and in this life whose sinful actions are completely eradicated and who are freed from duality of delusion engage themselves in my service with determination.
Who is speaking these words? Krishna.
He has engaged in lot of pious activities.
If we are engaged in lot of papa, then we become more and more disqualified to understand God.
When we engage in punya, good activities, we become qualified to understand about the Supreme Lord.
And one whose sinful reactions are eradicated and who is freed from dvandamoha, nirmukta, he will engage in my devotional service.
He will worship me with determination.
Persons who have acted piously in previous life and in this life whose sinful actions are completely eradicated and who are freed from duality of delusion.
So, one time, one devotee asked Lord Prabhupada, does that mean that we are chanting the names of God, we are worshipping Krishna?
Does that mean we have done all this?
We have engaged in lot of pious activities.
We have engaged in all the sinful reactions, all burnt away.
And, therefore, we are able to now worship the Supreme Lord.
The answer is, just like a man can become a billionaire or a millionaire, broadly speaking, two ways.
One is, he accumulates money and over many years, maybe, he accumulates enough and he can be called a millionaire.
So, the Vedic process, you know, what is the Vedic process?
The Vedic process, you know, what is the Vedic process?
The Vedic process, you know, what is the Vedic process?
Over many, many lifetimes, we accumulate Punya.
We go to places of pilgrimage, accumulate piety, get some Agyat, Sukruti.
And then, we come to a stage where we understand the Supreme Lord and come out of this Dvandva Moha.
There is another way of becoming rich.
What is that way of becoming rich?
So, when we come in touch with pure devotee of the Lord, actually, we get the lottery.
He is very, the pure devotee of the Lord is very kind, very magnanimous.
He wants to give this knowledge.
More than our desire to get this knowledge, it is his desire to give that knowledge.
Just like Bill Gates has decided, okay, 22 billion dollars I will give in charity.
He will find ways and means where to give.
So, likewise, a pure devotee of the Lord, he is...
And Lord has sent such pure devotees, representatives in this world where his sons are rotting in delusion.
Yada yadahi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata.
When that understanding is lacking, when people are suffering, dharma is lost.
I personally come and send my representatives.
When this understanding of what is this material world, why we are suffering, why we are rotting is lost,
then the Lord personally comes and sends his representatives.
And by the mercy of such pure devotees, our good fortune opens.
We can come to the understanding of what is our true position.
And we can come to that position which Krishna is talking about.
Then we can accept devotional service and gradually rise to pure knowledge of the supreme personality of Godhead.
This elevation is possible only by association of pure devotees who can deliver one from such delusion.
Once there was a small little girl.
He met a person by name John Ruskin.
He was a very eminent scholar, philosopher.
He was also a very accomplished painter.
So when this girl met, you know, just as a small parting gift,
he gifted this girl a small little handkerchief, silk handkerchief.
And it was, you know, a very fond gift.
She was very fond of this.
And whenever she would go, because after all it's John Ruskin who was given, not an ordinary person.
One day while she was playing with her brother,
the ink from an ink pot, a drop of ink fell on her.
The drop of ink fell on that handkerchief and there was a blot.
And she was very, very upset.
And she was, you know, people tried to say that doesn't matter, we'll wash it.
But she was not ready to listen.
Somehow as a last resort, the father took that girl back to John Ruskin,
took an appointment and said, see this girl is not willing to budge.
Can you give a different handkerchief?
So this John Ruskin took the handkerchief and then using that, you know, his paintbrush.
He just put some brushes under this thing.
And that blot of ink soon transformed into a flower.
Same dot, but then all around he made the petals and all that.
And same dot became part of a flower.
And the girl seeing that was very, very thrilled.
There was no longer a blot.
But now it was a very beautiful flower in the same very handkerchief.
Now what's the learning from this story?
Can anyone tell me with an answer?
You can transform your own situations.
Something very close to the right answer?
If you stay in the light, then you will be more, more happy.
In any suffering, there is a kind of positivity.
How do you look at the same thing?
Is it a blot or is it a flower?
If you get into, get in touch with the God, then you can transform your life into a flower.
We are dirty to different degrees.
But when we come into the light.
But when we come in touch with a pure devotee of the Lord, we can transform our heart.
And all those blots which are there, all those sinful reactions, just stinking, it can get transformed into a fragrant flower.
And that's what a pure devotee does.
Devotees of the Lord traverse this earth just to recover the conditioned soul.
Intelligent person who are endeavouring for liberation from old age and death take refuge in me in devotional service.
They are actually Brahman because they entirely know everything about transcendental and fruitive activities.
Now, persons who are endeavouring for liberation from old age and death, all of us don't want to die.
Suddenly we see some fire or smoke in this hall and feel something else and we will all rush out.
In fact, if we see everything is locked, we will jump from the windows.
In the World Trade Centre, people jumped from the 70th floor.
The building was on fire and you just popped out of the window.
They didn't want to die because of fire.
They knew that they may not survive but still take that chance.
We don't want to become old.
These days, especially we have anti-wrinkle creams and anti-aging cosmetics but then they have limitations.
So, old age, disease, intelligent person.
So, it's not wrong to avoid death.
Soul is not designed to die.
Intelligent person who are endeavouring for liberation from old age and death take refuge in me.
Because they entirely know everything about transcendental and fruitive activities.
Both death, old age and diseases affect this material body but not the spirit soul.
I am that eternal spirit soul.
Just because today we are in ignorance, we have false ego, we have identified with the body, we experience diseases, old age, death.
A pure devotee of the Lord who is completely aloof, he will go through old age.
But he has no fear of death.
He has, he transcends that pain.
There is a difference between pain and the experience of pain.
It is not that when a pure devotee has disease, he will have pain but he will not experience that.
He will not experience that pain.
I gave this example quite often.
Indian team is winning or losing but it does not affect me.
Indian team is winning or losing but it does not because I have not identified.
I am transcendental to all that.
When one engages in eternal devotional service with the understanding Aham Dhammasmi,
Those who know me as the Supreme Lord, as the governing principle of the material manifestation,
who know me as one underlying all the demigods and as one sustaining all sacrifices,
can with steadfast mind understand and know me even at the time of death.
One can understand how the Supreme Lord is the governing principle of material manifestation.
The beginning of this Krishna consciousness is association of a person who are Krishna conscious.
Only a person who has that consciousness of the Lord, whose eyes are open, Tatva Darshi,
when we come in touch with that person,
he has the ability to open our eyes.
Om Agyana Timiran Dasya Gyananjana Shalakya.
Because his eyes are open, he has the ability to open our eyes.
Such association is spiritual and puts one directly in touch with the Supreme Lord.
Narada Muni, as I mentioned, I think last class also, his previous life he was a son of a maid servant.
But fortunately, during Chaturmas, he had an opportunity to hear the discourses which these sages were giving.
And because a mother was a maid servant, he would also assist her mother in serving these devotees.
And he was fortunate to take the remnants of the food, Prasad, after they had taken the food.
And because he served them unconditionally, he took the Mahaprasad, he heard what they were discussing,
And it says that next life, he became an exalted Narada Muni.
So when we contact pure devotees of the Lord, knowingly or unknowingly, our good fortune starts.
Lav Matra Sadhu Sangha Sarva Siddhi Hoi.
But the point is, we have to contact a pure devotee, who himself is conscious of the Lord,
By his grace, one can understand Krishna to be the Supreme Lord.
Krishna says, they worship me with determination.
At the time of death, and when we practice this understanding, we practice this process,
by the mercy of a pure devotee, at the point of death, we will remember the Lord.
And if we remember the Lord at the point of death, we will remember the Lord.
And if we remember the Lord at the point of death, what happens?
By the way, let me ask you this question before we end.
We all want to be successful.
Some of us think that if we have lot of money, that is success.
Somebody says, if I can do lot of good work in this world, and people remember me after I am gone,
I leave a legacy, then that is success.
So, the answer is Anta Narayana Smriti.
The real success is if we can remember the Lord and leave this body.
Now, you may say it is very easy thing.
When that point comes, I will remember somehow.
It says what we remember at that time is cumulative.
Cumulative of what we have remembered throughout our lives.
Cumulative effect of what we have remembered, what we have thought of in this world.
So, at the point of yamyam vapi smaran bhavam tyajati ante kalevaram.
Whatever our state of mind or consciousness at the point of death, that state we attain in our next life.
So, if we are able to remember the Lord and in consciousness of the Lord, in full consciousness of the Lord,
we leave this body, which will happen if we have become purified our desires.
If we have developed attachment to the Lord, which is the required qualification to go back to.
If we have unfulfilled desires, based on that we will have one kind of remembrance and based on that we get the next body.
So long as we have material desires, we will get different material bodies.
And so long as we have material bodies, we will have janma, mrityu, jara, yadi.
So, if we want to get out of this, we don't want to die.
The solution is not Columbia Asia Hospital.
The solution is not Global Hospital.
The solution is to come out of this cycle of transmigration and go back to our original home, the Kingdom of God.
